# Duravel Reference Program — Levi Loveless

*Engine-derived reference case. Generated by tracing the deterministic engine
(`buildSkeleton`) for Levi's exact profile against the post-Batch-1–6 code, then
auditing the output against the Section 6 target. This is the structural
backbone the AI fill layer dresses with specific exercises, station
prescriptions, and paces in the live app.*

---

## 1. Profile → how the engine reads it

| Input | Value | Engine mapping |
|---|---|---|
| Event | HYROX, **pro / solo** | `sport: "hyrox"`, pro division → heavier station loads at fill time |
| Race date | **Nov 21, 2026** | 17-week program (starting wk of Jul 27); race in week 17 |
| Budget | **15 h/week** | band `h10_20` → drives volume, zones, session cap, strength dose |
| Training class | competitive/pro | `highly_trained` → 4-week microcycle, base-heavy 8/6/4/2 anchor |
| Lifting | **advanced** | top of the strength-dose range for the band (3 lifts/wk) |
| Hybrid | **intermediate** | 1–2 hybrid sessions/phase |
| Running | **beginner** | fewest run *slots* per phase (but see the volume flag in §6) |
| Days | **7/week** | full week available; no forced rest days |
| Prefs | long run Sat, doubles Sat/Sun | long run pinned to Saturday; a second session stacks on the weekend |
| Age | 27 | not masters → standard deload frequency |
| Bodyweight | **250 → 220 lb** by race | weight-loss context (see injury-load note, §6) |
| 5k | **26:00** (~8:22/mi) | anchors the pace bands below |

**A-race** priority ⇒ a full 2-week taper.

---

## 2. Program shape (17 weeks)

Phase allocation for a `highly_trained` athlete over 17 weeks with an A-race
taper resolves to:

| Phase | Weeks | Microcycle labels (4-wk pattern: rebound → increase → increase → deload) |
|---|---|---|
| **Base** | 1–7 | reb, inc, inc, deload, reb, inc, inc |
| **Build** | 8–12 | deload, reb, inc, inc, deload |
| **Peak** | 13–15 | reb, inc, inc |
| **Taper** | 16–17 | taper, **race (wk 17)** |

**Starting volume (band `h10_20`):** 37 running miles / **666 cardio minutes**
per week (≈ 11 h of cardio; the remaining budget is strength). Week 1 holds the
start; increase weeks add the greater of +1.5 mi / +7.5 % (capped at +10 %) and
+20 min / +10 % cardio; deloads drop to 60 % without lowering the held peak.

---

## 3. Weekly session architecture (the reference structure)

Session **counts** come from the phase/experience tables, then the **session
cap** (`h10_20` = 8) trims easy filler first so the week lands on ~5–6 quality
anchors, not 8–10 fragments. The **strength dose** is 3 lifts (advanced lifter at
the top of the band's 2–3 range), split **2 heavy + 1 power** — never more than 2
heavy. The weekly **long run is a compromised long run** (stations threaded in)
every non-race week.

| Phase | Runs | Lifts | Hybrids | Total | The anchors |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Base** | 3 | 3 | 1 | **7** | long-compromised, threshold, **VO2**; 2 heavy + 1 power lift; 1 hybrid |
| **Build** | 3 | 3 | 2 | **8** | long-compromised, threshold, **VO2**; 2 heavy + 1 power; 2 hybrids |
| **Peak** | 3 | 3 | 2 | **8** | long-compromised, threshold, **VO2**; 2 heavy + 1 power; 2 hybrids (1 = **full race simulation**) |
| **Taper** | 2 | 2 | 0 | ~4 | long-compromised, **VO2**; 1 heavy + 1 power. Race wk = shakeout + opener + race |
| *Deload wks* | 3 | 2 | 1 | ~6 | intensity held, volume −40 % (shorter sessions, not fewer anchors) |

**Zone distribution** (engine 5-zone, and the research 3-zone it rolls up to).
Intensity migrates up through the phases while easy volume stays dominant:

| Phase | Z1 | Z2 | Z3 | Z4 | Z5 | → easy / gray / **hard** |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Base | 21 | 63 | 5 | 3 | 8 | 84 / 8 / **8** |
| Build | 20 | 61 | 5 | 3 | 11 | 81 / 8 / **11** |
| Peak | 19 | 56 | 5 | 3 | 17 | 75 / 8 / **17** |
| Taper | 20 | 60 | 5 | 3 | 12 | 80 / 8 / **12** |

Program-weighted hard ≈ **11 %** — right on the band's 12 % target, with a
genuine peak-phase spike to 17 %. VO2 and threshold appear **every single week**,
Base included.

---

## 4. Representative Build week (illustrative day grid)

Placement rules applied: long run pinned to Saturday; **no two lifts on one
day**; every hard-leg (full/power) lift **paired with easy cardio** the same day;
heavy legs kept off the day before a key run; weekend doubles honored. Paces from
the 26:00 5k.

| Day | Session(s) | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| **Mon** | Heavy strength (full) + easy run | 2 heavy = squat/hinge/press ~4×4–5 @ 83 %; easy 30–40 min @ ~10:30/mi |
| **Tue** | **VO2 intervals** | 6–8 × 800 m @ ~8:00/mi, 1 min jog recovery |
| **Wed** | Power lift + easy Z2 cardio | trap-bar/jump work, explosive intent, full recovery; 30 min easy bike/row |
| **Thu** | **Threshold run** | 20–30 min @ ~8:50/mi (comfortably hard) |
| **Fri** | Heavy strength (full) | second heavy day; posterior-chain + carries emphasis |
| **Sat** | **Long compromised run** (double) | 60–90 min Z1–3; every ~10–15 min break for a station (sled, lunges, burpees, ski/row) |
| **Sun** | Hybrid / HYROX + optional easy spin | race-pace compromised running between 3–4 stations |

*(Exact exercises, station loads for pro division, and prescriptions are filled
by the AI layer in the live app; the engine owns the structure above.)*

---

## 5. Station priorities from your March 2026 DC result

Your doubles splits (lower field-rank = stronger) point the strength/hybrid work
where it pays off most:

- **Strengths — maintain:** Row (#247), Wall Balls (#360), Lunges (#368), SkiErg (#364).
- **Weaknesses — target:** **Farmers Carry (#637)**, **Sled Pull (#622)**, **Burpee Broad Jump (#558)**. That's a grip / loaded-carry / posterior-chain and conditioning-under-fatigue cluster — well matched to the 2 heavy days (deadlift, rows, carries, RDLs) plus the compromised-run burpee/broad-jump exposure.
- **Running is the clear limiter** (beginner, 26:00 5k; running legs were your slowest phase). The whole-program VO2-every-week + threshold-every-week structure is aimed straight at this.

Turning on the engine's **needs analysis** (optional) would bias the plan further
toward running and these three stations automatically.

---

## 6. Section 6 audit — what the engine now nails, and two residual risks

**Matches the research (post Batch 1–6):**

- ✅ **~5–6 anchors, not 10 fragments** — the 15 h week lands at 7–8 sessions (3 quality runs + 3 lifts + 1–2 hybrids), where the old model produced 10.
- ✅ **VO2 + threshold are weekly anchors in every phase**, Base included.
- ✅ **Budget-scaled intensity** — hard fraction ~11 % program-average, peaking at 17 %, matching the 15 h band rather than being clamped low.
- ✅ **Research strength dose** — 3 lifts (advanced → top of the 2–3 band range), **2 heavy + 1 power**, never >2 heavy; power persists into Peak/Taper.
- ✅ **One weight session per day**, hard-leg lifts **paired with easy cardio**.
- ✅ **The long compromised run** is present as the named weekly keystone.

**Two residual risks this reference case surfaces (recommend a Batch 7):**

1. **Beginner-runner volume is band-driven, not experience-scaled.** The band
   starts running at **37 mi/week regardless of run experience**, while the run
   *count* stays at the beginner level (3). Result: ~37 mi concentrated into only
   3 runs, forcing a very long individual long run. For a **beginner runner**
   that ramp and that single-run length is aggressive. *Fix option:* scale
   `bandStartMileage` down by running experience, or let a beginner exceed the
   session cap with additional short easy runs (more frequency, less per-run
   load).

2. **Impact load for a 250 lb athlete cutting to 220.** The impact guardrail
   (routing surplus aerobic volume to low-impact cardio) only engages at the
   `h20_30`+ bands, so at 15 h Levi carries **all 666 cardio minutes as running
   impact** at high bodyweight. *Fix option:* make the low-impact routing
   bodyweight-aware, or expose a "high-impact-sensitive" flag that shifts some Z1
   volume to bike/row/ski during the weight-cut phase.

Neither is a correctness bug — both are calibration choices that this specific,
realistic profile (heavy, strong lifter, novice runner, high budget) pushes into
the open. They're the natural next tuning targets.

---

## 7. Next steps

- If you want **byte-exact** per-week mileage/cardio numbers and the full 17-week
  day grid, run the one-file spec on your machine (it builds this same skeleton
  and writes JSON) — I kept it staged as `lib/engine/_levi_ref.ts.bak`; rename to
  `_levi_ref.test.ts` and `npm test -- _levi_ref` to dump it.
- Say the word and I'll wire **Batch 7** (experience-scaled beginner volume +
  bodyweight-aware impact routing) the same build-verified way as Batches 1–6.
- To generate the **full athlete-facing plan** (exact exercises, pro-division
  station loads, per-run paces), the live app's AI fill layer runs on top of this
  structure.
